nodes.title: Ashes Beneath Silk
body: The Han court still stands, but authority has begun to fracture into warlords, private armies and local granaries.
You decide to answer collapse with structure before your rivals can define the north.

choices.label: Deploy Vanguard
choices.subtext: Secure initiative before the court stalls you.

nodes.title: Council of Methods
body: After two deployments, your officers demand direction: terror, doctrine or calculated alliances.
Your answer will determine where pressure will be applied to next

choices.label: Public Supression
choices.subtext: Cull resistance openly to establish obedience.

choices.label: Doctrine Enforcement
choices.subtext: Reform command ranks and enforce discipline through execution orders.

choices.label: Co-opt the Militias
choices.subtext: Absorb local cliques under your banner while they still fear exposure

nodes.title: Order by Fear
body: You order searches at road junctions and executions at granary towns to make an example of dissenters.
Compliance rises quickly; as does resentment. Your forces are now feared, but also watched for signs of oppurtunity

nodes.title: Rule by Code
body: You restructure command with formal regulations, rotating officers before they form cliques and factions
Discipline hardens, but every decree now requires enforcement under heavy surveillance.

nodes.title: Order by Absorption
body: You asorb militia leaders with stable rank and pay. Their men now march ever stronger under supply rules you control.
The current arrangement is useful but unstable. You must act before they can leverage their local influence against you.

choices.label: Press the Palace Line
choices.subtext: Convert fresh loyalties into pure obedience.

nodes.title: The Emperor's Shadow
body: With regional resistance and warlords crushed, the final question is political lineage: guard the throne, control it, or seize your destiny as emperor.
(Each path carries a different war doctrine into Act I).

choices.label: Protect the Emperor
choices.subtext: Claim authority through guardianship and formal legitimacy.

choices.label: Control the Emperor
choices.subtext: Use the throne to project command and authority.

choices.label: Seize the Throne
choices.subtext: Build extra-institutional authority outside the court via logistics and field coercion.

nodes.title: Chancellor's Leverage
body: You declare protection of the throne as state doctrine, forcing rivals to challenge you as rebels, not peers.

nodes.title: Chancellor's Leverage
body: You control petitions, appointments, and proclamations. Opposition must now fight both armies and paperwork.

nodes.title: Field Sovereignty
body: You abandon court symbolism and tie rule directly to roads, grain and officer obedience. A new empire is in the making from the blood of the old.
